meta_title: For Students: Antigravity project template to Solve no marketing skills (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome no marketing skills. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Antigravity Template to Solve No Marketing Skills
content.seo_description: Overcome the lack of marketing skills with the Antigravity project template. A beginner-friendly checklist for students using Claude Code to build apps.
content.answer_block: You don't need a marketing degree to launch; you need an automated distribution engine. By using the Antigravity project template, you bake SEO-optimized metadata, open-graph tags, and viral loops directly into your codebase. Use Claude Code to scaffold these features, turning your technical product into a self-marketing machine.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks perfecting your codebase, only to realize that a brilliant application is invisible without a marketing strategy. The silence from your launch is deafening, and the fear of 'building in a vacuum' is a rite of passage every developer knows. Marketing feels like a foreign language—noisy, abstract, and entirely removed from the clean logic of your IDE.

But here is the secret: engineers don't need to be marketers; they need to be architects of traction. The Antigravity project template bridges this gap by embedding growth triggers directly into your application's architecture. Stop chasing clicks and start building systems that pull users in automatically.

steps.title: Define Your Value Proposition
steps.description: Before writing code, use Antigravity to generate a concise, user-centric value statement that dictates your app's SEO metadata.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my project idea and generate 5 punchy value propositions, a 160-character meta description, and a list of 10 high-intent keywords for SEO targeting.

steps.title: Implement Viral Open-Graph Tags
steps.description: Ensure your app looks premium when shared on social media by auto-generating rich preview cards.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a reusable React component for Open Graph and Twitter card meta tags that dynamically pulls the page title and description from the current route.

steps.title: Automate User Onboarding Loops
steps.description: Reduce churn by building an automated email sequence that triggers upon user sign-up, keeping them engaged without manual work.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a trigger-based email onboarding flow for new users. Generate the template logic and identify the API hooks needed for integration.

steps.title: Integrate Analytics Hooks
steps.description: You cannot optimize what you cannot measure. Inject tracking events into your core user actions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Identify the 5 most critical user actions in my app and generate code to track these events via PostHog or Google Analytics.

steps.title: Deploy with Built-in SEO
steps.description: Finalize your production build with a sitemap and robots.txt generation configured to maximize search engine discoverability.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a dynamic sitemap.xml and robots.txt file configuration for my project to ensure all public-facing routes are indexed.

pro_tips: Treat your README.md as a sales landing page; it is often the first thing people see on your GitHub.
Build a 'Public Roadmap' page inside your app to turn users into stakeholders who feel invested in your success.
Use Claude Code to refactor your error pages into helpful, brand-aligned 'oops' moments that encourage users to try again.
Automate your changelog: a product that updates frequently signals reliability and keeps users coming back.
Embed a 'Share this result' button on every successful action to turn every user interaction into a potential marketing asset.
content.next_steps: After completing this checklist, iterate on your user feedback loop. Monitor your analytics for one week, identify where users drop off, and use Antigravity to refactor those specific friction points.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical foundation to stop being a ghost in the machine and start building a product that markets itself. The barrier between you and your first 1,000 users is just one deployment away. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
